posts.content: 我作为私生子出生在芬奇镇。既然正统的学术之路对我关门，我便拜大自然为师。我整日观察蜻蜓的飞行与流水的旋涡，用双眼学习这世界的语言。
posts.translation: I was born out of wedlock in Vinci. Forbidden from formal scholarly paths, I turned to the greatest teacher of all: Nature. I spent my days watching the flight of dragonflies and the swirl of currents, learning the language of the world through my own eyes.

posts.content: 在佛罗伦萨，我进入了韦罗基奥的作坊。当旁人只看到木板上的油彩时，我看到的是光的力学与人体的结构。画出一张脸是为了理解灵魂，而要理解灵魂，必先精通骨骼与肌肉。
posts.translation: In Florence, I joined Verrocchio’s workshop. While others saw only paint on wood, I saw the mechanics of light and the architecture of the human form. To paint a face is to understand the soul, and to understand the soul, one must first master the bone and the muscle.

posts.content: 我离开佛罗伦萨前往米兰。我给斯福尔扎大公写了一封信，承诺为他制造战争机器、装甲坦克和便携式桥梁——最后才轻描淡写地提到，我也能画画，不输给任何人。
posts.translation: I left Florence for Milan. I sent a letter to Duke Sforza promising him war machines, armored tanks, and portable bridges—and mentioned, almost as an afterthought, that I could also paint as well as any other.

posts.content: 米兰的僧侣们看着我画《最后的晚餐》，一画就是好几年。我不仅是在画一场晚宴，而是在定格一场心理爆发的瞬间。每一个手势、每一道阴影、每一抹惊恐的眼神，都是我对情感与几何的研究。
posts.translation: The monks in Milan watched me for years as I worked on 'The Last Supper.' I was not just painting a meal; I was freezing a moment of psychological explosion. Every gesture, every shadow, and every panicked glance was a study in human emotion and geometry.

posts.content: 我开始为一位佛罗伦萨贵妇画像。我用‘晕涂法’模糊了边缘，就像烟雾消散在空气中。生命本没有生硬的线条，万物相连。那个微笑——她是在看我，还是在看那些我尚未解开的谜团？
posts.translation: I began a portrait of a Florentine lady. I used 'sfumato' to blur the edges, much like how smoke dissolves into the air. Life has no hard lines; everything is connected. That smile—she is looking at me, or perhaps, at the mysteries I have yet to solve.

posts.content: 我的笔记本已经塞满了。飞行器、战争机器、心脏瓣膜图。世人称我为艺术家，但我只是实验的门徒。真知源于观察，余者皆为妄议。
posts.translation: My notebooks are overflowing. Flying machines, war engines, maps of the heart’s valves. People call me an artist, but I am simply a disciple of experience. To know is to see; everything else is mere opinion.

posts.content: 我在帕维亚的停尸房里度过了无数深夜，剥开皮肤与肌肉的层叠。我想看清一个微笑是如何构成的，心脏又是如何输送生命的。对我而言，人体是曾被设计出的最神圣的工程学杰作。
posts.translation: I spent late nights in the morgues of Pavia, peeling back layers of skin and muscle. I wanted to see how a smile is built and how the heart pumps life. To me, the human body is the most divine piece of engineering ever devised.

posts.content: 法兰索瓦一世邀请我前往法国，称我为“国王的首席画家、工程师与建筑师”。他赐予我一座庄园，更重要的是，给予了我白日做梦、在夕阳下空谈哲学的自由。
posts.translation: King Francis I invited me to France, calling me 'the premier painter, engineer, and architect to the King.' He gave me a manor and, more importantly, the freedom to dream and talk philosophy until the sun goes down.

posts.content: 批评者抱怨我留下了太多半成品。他们不明白，对我来说，探索理解的过程本身就是艺术。一旦谜团在脑中解开，画布便只是这场旅途留下的遗迹。
posts.translation: My critics complain that I leave too much unfinished. They don't understand that for me, the struggle to understand is the art. Once a mystery is solved in my mind, the canvas is just a relic of the journey.

posts.content: 我在克劳斯·吕斯城堡的国王怀中咽下了最后一口气。我唯一的遗憾是，因为我的作品未能达到应有的高度而冒犯了上帝与世人。但看看我的草图吧——流水依然奔涌，飞鸟仍在翱翔。
posts.translation: I draw my final breath at Clos Lucé in the arms of the King. My only regret is that I have offended God and mankind because my work did not reach the quality it should have. But look at my sketches—the water still flows, the birds still soar.
